revision.comment: 新しいページ：下諏訪青塚古墳（長野県諏訪地方にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 青塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚3340 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|4|34.97|N|138|5|20.68|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=下諏訪青塚古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長57m |築造年代 = [[6世紀]]後半から末頃 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = 人物埴輪・[[円筒埴輪]] |史跡指定 = 長野県指定史跡「下諏訪青塚古墳」 |特記事項 = }} {{Location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=4|lat_sec=34.97|lon_deg=138|lon_min=5|lon_sec=20.68|label=下諏訪青塚古墳|position=center|width=150|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|label=|caption={{Center|下諏訪青塚古墳の位置}}}} '''下諏訪青塚古墳'''（しもすわあおづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚にある[[前方後円墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円墳である。 == 概要 == [[諏訪湖]]の北方、西に突き出すの丘陵突端部に位置する。[[諏訪大社]]下社秋宮付近にあり、現在も諏訪大社の社有地に含まれる[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/navi/kyouiku/bunkazai/aozukakofun.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。 墳形は諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円形で、前方部を北北西に向ける。前方後円墳としては末期の部類に属する{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。現在までに墳丘（特に東側）は周囲の開発によって削られているほか、くびれ部西側には諏訪大社末社の青塚社が建てられている。古墳の麓からは[[円筒埴輪]]が発見されているが、埴輪が出土した点においてもこの青塚古墳が諏訪地方で唯一の例になる。[[江戸時代]]の史料によると武人埴輪が出土し埋め戻されたというが、現在は円筒埴輪以外は見つかっていない。 埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部西側のくびれ部付近、基準面から5メートルほどの高さに露出・開口する現地説明板。。[[安山岩]]の自然石による乱石積からなり、石室全長は5.5メートルを測る[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/navi/kyouiku/bunkazai/aozukakofun.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。羨道は破壊されているが、両袖式の石室になると推測される{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。この石室からの出土品はほとんど知られていない。 以上の墳丘・石室の形態や出土埴輪により、古墳は[[6世紀]]後半から末頃の築造と推定される。その築造を巡っては、諏訪大社祭祀氏族との関係のほか、[[天竜川]]沿いの前方後円墳との関係が指摘される。 古墳域は、[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に長野県指定史跡に指定された。 File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|{{small|くびれ部に青塚社（左）、後円部に石室（右上）}} File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|石室開口部 == 規模 == * 墳丘長：57メートル規模の記載は[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）による。現地説明板では主軸長67メートルとする。 * 後円部 ** 直径：34メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8.1メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8メートル == 文化財 == === 長野県指定史跡 === * 下諏訪青塚古墳 - 昭和40年2月25日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=青塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=9784582490204|ref={{Harvid|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/navi/kyouiku/bunkazai/aozukakofun.html 青塚古墳] - 下諏訪町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:しもすわあおつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]] [[Category:諏訪地域]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 青塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚3340 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|4|34.97|N|138|5|20.68|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=下諏訪青塚古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長57m（67m?） |築造年代 = [[7世紀]]末から[[8世紀]]頃
または[[6世紀]]後半から末頃 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]、（伝）人物埴輪 |史跡指定 = 長野県指定史跡「下諏訪青塚古墳」 |特記事項 = }} {{Location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=4|lat_sec=34.97|lon_deg=138|lon_min=5|lon_sec=20.68|label=下諏訪青塚古墳|position=center|width=150|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|label=|caption={{Center|下諏訪青塚古墳の位置}}}} '''下諏訪青塚古墳'''（しもすわあおづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚にある[[前方後円墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円墳である。 == 概要 == [[諏訪湖]]北方の下諏訪地域において、西に突き出す丘陵の突端部に位置する。[[諏訪大社]]下社秋宮の近くに所在し、古墳域は諏訪大社の社有地に含まれる[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/navi/kyouiku/bunkazai/aozukakofun.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。 墳形は諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円形で、前方部を北北西に向ける。前方後円墳としては末期に属する形態である{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。現在までに墳丘（特に東側）は周囲の開発によって削られているほか、くびれ部西側には諏訪大社境外社の青塚社が建てられている。古墳の麓からは[[円筒埴輪]]が発見されているが、埴輪が出土した点においてもこの青塚古墳が諏訪地方で唯一の例になる。[[江戸時代]]の史料によると武人埴輪が出土し埋め戻されたというが、近年の調査では円筒埴輪以外は見つかっていない。 埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部西側のくびれ部付近、基準面から5メートルほどの高さに露出・開口する現地説明板。。[[安山岩]]の自然石による乱石積からなり、石室全長は5.5メートルを測る[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/navi/kyouiku/bunkazai/aozukakofun.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。羨道は破壊されているが、両袖式の石室になると推測される{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。この石室からの出土品はほとんど知られていない。 以上の墳丘・石室の形態や出土埴輪に基づく推定築造年代は、[[7世紀]]末から[[8世紀]]頃、または[[6世紀]]後半から末頃と諸説がある。その築造を巡っては、諏訪大社下社の祭祀氏族（金刺氏）との関係のほか、[[天竜川]]沿いに分布する前方後円墳との関係が指摘される。 古墳域は、[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に長野県指定史跡に指定された。 File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|{{small|くびれ部に青塚社（左）、後円部に石室（右上）}} File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|石室開口部 == 規模 == * 墳丘長：57メートル規模の記載は[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）による。 * 後円部 ** 直径：34メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8.1メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8メートル ただし現地説明板では、主軸長67メートル、後円部直径33.4メートル、前方部幅42.8メートルとしている。 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定史跡 === * 下諏訪青塚古墳 - 昭和40年2月25日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=青塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=9784582490204|ref={{Harvid|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/navi/kyouiku/bunkazai/aozukakofun.html 青塚古墳] - 下諏訪町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:しもすわあおつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]] [[Category:諏訪地域]]

revision.comment: テンプレートに地図移動。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 青塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚3340 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 34.97 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 5|経度秒 = 20.68 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長57m（67m?） |築造年代 = [[7世紀]]末から[[8世紀]]頃
または[[6世紀]]後半から末頃 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]、（伝）人物埴輪 |史跡指定 = 長野県指定史跡「下諏訪青塚古墳」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 }} '''下諏訪青塚古墳'''（しもすわあおづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚にある[[前方後円墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円墳である。 == 概要 == [[諏訪湖]]北方の下諏訪地域において、西に突き出す丘陵の突端部に位置する。[[諏訪大社]]下社秋宮の近くに所在し、古墳域は諏訪大社の社有地に含まれる[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/navi/kyouiku/bunkazai/aozukakofun.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。 墳形は諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円形で、前方部を北北西に向ける。前方後円墳としては末期に属する形態である{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。現在までに墳丘（特に東側）は周囲の開発によって削られているほか、くびれ部西側には諏訪大社境外社の青塚社が建てられている。古墳の麓からは[[円筒埴輪]]が発見されているが、埴輪が出土した点においてもこの青塚古墳が諏訪地方で唯一の例になる。[[江戸時代]]の史料によると武人埴輪が出土し埋め戻されたというが、近年の調査では円筒埴輪以外は見つかっていない。 埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部西側のくびれ部付近、基準面から5メートルほどの高さに露出・開口する現地説明板。。[[安山岩]]の自然石による乱石積からなり、石室全長は5.5メートルを測る[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/navi/kyouiku/bunkazai/aozukakofun.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。羨道は破壊されているが、両袖式の石室になると推測される{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。この石室からの出土品はほとんど知られていない。 以上の墳丘・石室の形態や出土埴輪に基づく推定築造年代は、[[7世紀]]末から[[8世紀]]頃、または[[6世紀]]後半から末頃と諸説がある。その築造を巡っては、諏訪大社下社の祭祀氏族（金刺氏）との関係のほか、[[天竜川]]沿いに分布する前方後円墳との関係が指摘される。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|{{small|くびれ部に青塚社（左）、後円部に石室（右上）}} File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|石室開口部 == 規模 == * 墳丘長：57メートル規模の記載は[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）による。 * 後円部 ** 直径：34メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8.1メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8メートル ただし現地説明板では、主軸長67メートル、後円部直径33.4メートル、前方部幅42.8メートルとしている。 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定史跡 === * 下諏訪青塚古墳 - 昭和40年2月25日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=青塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=9784582490204|ref={{Harvid|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/navi/kyouiku/bunkazai/aozukakofun.html 青塚古墳] - 下諏訪町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:しもすわあおつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]] [[Category:諏訪地域]]

墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚3340 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 34.97 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 5|経度秒 = 20.68 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長57m（または67m） |築造年代 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]・（伝）人物埴輪 |史跡指定 = 長野県指定史跡「下諏訪青塚古墳」 |特記事項 = |ラベル = 下諏訪青塚
古墳 |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 }} '''下諏訪青塚古墳'''（しもすわあおづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円墳である。 == 概要 == [[諏訪湖]]北方の下諏訪地域において、西に突き出す丘陵の突端部に位置する。[[諏訪大社]]下社秋宮の近くに所在し、古墳域は諏訪大社の社有地に含まれる。 墳形は諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円形で、前方部を北北西に向ける。前方後円墳としては末期に属する形態である{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。現在までに墳丘（特に東側）は周囲の開発によって削られているほか、くびれ部西側には諏訪大社境外社の青塚社が建てられている。古墳の麓からは[[円筒埴輪]]が発見されているが、埴輪が出土した点においてもこの青塚古墳が諏訪地方で唯一の例になる。[[江戸時代]]の史料によると武人埴輪が出土し埋め戻されたというが、近年の調査では円筒埴輪以外は見つかっていない。主体部の埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部西側のくびれ部付近、基準面から5メートルほどの高さに露出・開口する史跡説明板。。[[安山岩]]の自然石による乱石積からなり、石室全長は5.5メートルを測る[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。羨道は破壊されているが、両袖式の石室になると推測される{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。この石室からの出土品はほとんど知られていない。 この青塚古墳は、墳丘・石室の形態や出土埴輪から[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃の築造と推定される。その築造を巡っては、諏訪大社下社の祭祀氏族（金刺氏）との関係のほか、[[天竜川]]沿いに分布する前方後円墳との関係が指摘される。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 規模 == * 墳丘長：57メートル規模の記載は[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）による。 * 後円部 ** 直径：34メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8.1メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8メートル ただし現地説明板では、主軸長67メートル、後円部直径33.4メートル、前方部幅42.8メートルとする。 File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|くびれ部に青塚社（左）、後円部に石室（右上） File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|石室開口部 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 下諏訪青塚古墳 - 1965年（昭和40年）2月25日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=青塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳] - 下諏訪町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:しもすわあおつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]]

墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚3340 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 34.97 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 5|経度秒 = 20.68 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長57m（または67m） |築造年代 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]・（伝）人物埴輪 |史跡指定 = 長野県指定史跡「下諏訪青塚古墳」 |特記事項 = |ラベル = 下諏訪青塚
古墳 |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 }} '''下諏訪青塚古墳'''（しもすわあおづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円墳である。 == 概要 == [[諏訪湖]]北方の下諏訪地域において、西に突き出す丘陵の突端部に位置する。[[諏訪大社]]下社秋宮の近くに所在し、古墳域は諏訪大社の社有地に含まれる。 墳形は諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円形で、前方部を北北西に向ける。前方後円墳としては末期に属する形態である{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。現在までに墳丘（特に東側）は周囲の開発によって削られているほか、くびれ部西側には諏訪大社境外社の青塚社が建てられている。古墳の麓からは[[円筒埴輪]]が発見されているが、埴輪が出土した点においてもこの青塚古墳が諏訪地方で唯一の例になる。[[江戸時代]]の史料によると武人埴輪が出土し埋め戻されたというが、近年の調査では円筒埴輪以外は見つかっていない。主体部の埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部西側のくびれ部付近、基準面から5メートルほどの高さに露出・開口する史跡説明板。。[[安山岩]]の自然石による乱石積からなり、石室全長は5.5メートルを測る[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。羨道は破壊されているが、両袖式の石室になると推測される{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}。この石室からの出土品はほとんど知られていない。 この青塚古墳は、墳丘・石室の形態や出土埴輪から[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃の築造と推定される。その築造を巡っては、諏訪大社下社の祭祀氏族（金刺氏）との関係のほか、[[天竜川]]沿いに分布する前方後円墳との関係が指摘される。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 規模 == * 墳丘長：57メートル規模の記載は[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）による。 * 後円部 ** 直径：34メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8.1メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8メートル ただし現地説明板では、主軸長67メートル、後円部直径33.4メートル、前方部幅42.8メートルとする。 File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|くびれ部に青塚社（左）、後円部に石室（右上） File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|石室開口部 ファイル:下諏訪 青塚古墳 2007.04.13 - panoramio - alisa 1988 08 (7).jpg|青塚古墳　説明文 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 下諏訪青塚古墳 - 1965年（昭和40年）2月25日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=青塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] * [[金刺氏]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳] - 下諏訪町ホームページ {{諏訪信仰}} {{デフォルトソート:しもすわあおつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 下諏訪青塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚3340 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 34.97 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 5|経度秒 = 20.68 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長57m（または67m） |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]、（伝）人物埴輪 |史跡 = 長野県指定史跡「下諏訪青塚古墳」 |特記事項 = |ラベル = 下諏訪青塚
古墳 |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 }} '''下諏訪青塚古墳'''（しもすわあおづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[諏訪湖]]北方の下諏訪地域において、西に突き出す丘陵の突端部に築造された古墳である。[[諏訪大社]]下社秋宮付近に位置し、古墳域は諏訪大社の社有地に含まれる。現在までに墳丘（特に東側）は周囲の開発によって削られているほか、くびれ部西側には諏訪大社境外社の青塚社が建てられている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北北西に向ける。諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円墳で、前方後円墳としては末期に属する形態である{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]が認められるが、埴輪が出土した点でも本古墳が諏訪地方で唯一の例になる。[[江戸時代]]の史料によると武人埴輪が出土し埋め戻されたというが、近年の調査では円筒埴輪以外は見つかっていない。主体部の埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部西側のくびれ部付近、基準面から5メートルほどの高さに露出・開口する史跡説明板。。[[安山岩]]の自然石による乱石積からなり、石室全長は5.5メートルを測る[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。羨道は破壊されているが、両袖式の石室になると推測される{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}。石室の副葬品の出土はほとんど知られていない。 この青塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃の築造と推定される。諏訪大社下社の祭祀氏族（金刺氏）との関係のほか、[[天竜川]]沿いに分布する前方後円墳との関係を指摘する説が挙げられ、古代の諏訪地方の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 * 墳丘長：57メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：34メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8.1メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8メートル ただし現地説明板では、主軸長67メートル、後円部直径33.4メートル、前方部幅42.8メートルとする。 File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|くびれ部に青塚社（左）、後円部に石室（右上） File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|石室開口部 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 下諏訪青塚古墳 - 1965年（昭和40年）2月25日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=青塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] * [[金刺氏]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳] - 下諏訪町ホームページ {{諏訪信仰}} {{デフォルトソート:しもすわあおつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 下諏訪青塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚3340 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 34.97 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 5|経度秒 = 20.68 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長57m（または67m） |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]、（伝）人物埴輪 |史跡 = 長野県指定史跡「下諏訪青塚古墳」 |特記事項 = |ラベル = 下諏訪青塚
古墳 |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 }} '''下諏訪青塚古墳'''（しもすわあおづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[諏訪湖]]北方の下諏訪地域において、西に突き出す丘陵の突端部に築造された古墳である。[[諏訪大社]]下社秋宮付近に位置し、古墳域は諏訪大社の社有地に含まれる。現在までに墳丘（特に東側）は周囲の開発によって削られているほか、くびれ部西側には諏訪大社境外社の青塚社が建てられている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北北西に向ける。諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円墳で、前方後円墳としては末期に属する形態である{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]が認められるが、埴輪が出土した点でも本古墳が諏訪地方で唯一の例になる。[[江戸時代]]の史料によると武人埴輪が出土し埋め戻されたというが、近年の調査では円筒埴輪以外は見つかっていない。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部西側のくびれ部付近、基準面から5メートルほどの高さに露出・開口する史跡説明板。。[[安山岩]]の自然石による乱石積からなり、石室全長は5.5メートルを測る[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。羨道は破壊されているが、両袖式の石室になると推測される{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}。石室の副葬品の出土はほとんど知られていない。 この青塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃の築造と推定される。諏訪大社下社の祭祀氏族（金刺氏）との関係のほか、[[天竜川]]沿いに分布する前方後円墳との関係を指摘する説が挙げられ、古代の諏訪地方の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 * 墳丘長：57メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：34メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8.1メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8メートル ただし現地説明板では、主軸長67メートル、後円部直径33.4メートル、前方部幅42.8メートルとする。 File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|くびれ部に青塚社（左）、後円部に石室（右上） File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|石室開口部 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 下諏訪青塚古墳 - 1965年（昭和40年）2月25日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=青塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] * [[金刺氏]] *[[下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|下諏町の名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳] - 下諏訪町ホームページ {{諏訪信仰}} {{デフォルトソート:しもすわあおつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]]

==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound | Name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | Location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ** Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ** Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ** Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ** Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:new pages]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: standardizing kofun infobox
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | Location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:new pages]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: using mass replace to fix all faulty capitalizations of the image parameter forr infibox ancient site
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | Location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Individual Kofun]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]]
