{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. revision.comment: Text replacement - "q---qq" to "qqqqqqqqq"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. revision.comment: Text replacement - "|古墳}}" to "|古墳|1=Kofun}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 4 | Latitude seconds = 34.97 | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 5 | Longitude seconds = 20.68 | ISO = JP-20 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Scale = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | Construction period = Late 6th century | Burial facility = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | Excavated items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designated historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | Remarks = | Label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Burial Mound | Map = Japan Nagano | Icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its shape is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designated as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The burial mound was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped burial mound in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped burial mounds distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The burial mound was designated as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]]
