Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == other_designation == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated other_designation === * other_designation: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural designation = Nagano Prefecture-designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's information board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == other_designation == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated other_designation === * other_designation: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Information Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural other_designation = Nagano Prefecture-other_designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural other_designation = Nagano Prefecture-other_designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == other_other_designation == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated other_other_designation === * other_other_designation: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural other_designation = Nagano Prefecture-other_designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == other_other_designation == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated other_other_designation === * other_other_designation: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural other_designation = Nagano Prefecture-other_designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural other_designation = Nagano Prefecture-other_designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == other_designation == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated other_designation === * other_designation: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural other_designation = Nagano Prefecture-other_designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == other_designation == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated other_designation === * other_designation: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural other_designation = Nagano Prefecture-other_designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == other_designation == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated other_designation === * other_designation: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural other_designation = Nagano Prefecture-other_designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == other_designation == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated other_designation === * other_designation: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]

Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. revision.comment: Text replacement - "burial Facility" to "burial"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural other_designation = Nagano Prefecture-other_designation1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is other_designation1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == other_designation == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated other_designation === * other_designation: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods.
