This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|12=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|12=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|12=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft|12=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|12=draft|12=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft|12=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|12=draft|12=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|横穴式石室|" to "|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft|12=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|12=draft|12=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: 18 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:下諏訪青塚古墳 up to rev 97369725
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 下諏訪青塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚3340 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 34.97 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 5|経度秒 = 20.68 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長57m（または67m） |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]、（伝）人物埴輪 |史跡 = 長野県指定史跡「下諏訪青塚古墳」 |特記事項 = |ラベル = 下諏訪青塚
古墳 |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 }} '''下諏訪青塚古墳'''（しもすわあおづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[諏訪湖]]北方の下諏訪地域において、西に突き出す丘陵の突端部に築造された古墳である。[[諏訪大社]]下社秋宮付近に位置し、古墳域は諏訪大社の社有地に含まれる。現在までに墳丘（特に東側）は周囲の開発によって削られているほか、くびれ部西側には諏訪大社境外社の青塚社が建てられている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北北西に向ける。諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円墳で、前方後円墳としては末期に属する形態である{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]が認められるが、埴輪が出土した点でも本古墳が諏訪地方で唯一の例になる。[[江戸時代]]の史料によると武人埴輪が出土し埋め戻されたというが、近年の調査では円筒埴輪以外は見つかっていない。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部西側のくびれ部付近、基準面から5メートルほどの高さに露出・開口する史跡説明板。。[[安山岩]]の自然石による乱石積からなり、石室全長は5.5メートルを測る[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。羨道は破壊されているが、両袖式の石室になると推測される{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}。石室の副葬品の出土はほとんど知られていない。 この青塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃の築造と推定される。諏訪大社下社の祭祀氏族（金刺氏）との関係のほか、[[天竜川]]沿いに分布する前方後円墳との関係を指摘する説が挙げられ、古代の諏訪地方の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 * 墳丘長：57メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：34メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8.1メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8メートル ただし現地説明板では、主軸長67メートル、後円部直径33.4メートル、前方部幅42.8メートルとする。 File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|くびれ部に青塚社（左）、後円部に石室（右上） File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|石室開口部 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 下諏訪青塚古墳 - 1965年（昭和40年）2月25日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=青塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] * [[金刺氏]] *[[下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|下諏町の名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳] - 下諏訪町ホームページ {{諏訪信仰}} {{デフォルトソート:しもすわあおつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 下諏訪青塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚3340 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 34.97 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 5|経度秒 = 20.68 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長57m（または67m） |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]、（伝）人物埴輪 |史跡 = 長野県指定史跡「下諏訪青塚古墳」 |特記事項 = |ラベル = 下諏訪青塚
古墳 |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 }} '''下諏訪青塚古墳'''（しもすわあおづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]青塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[諏訪湖]]北方の下諏訪地域において、西に突き出す丘陵の突端部に築造された古墳である。[[諏訪大社]]下社秋宮付近に位置し、古墳域は諏訪大社の社有地に含まれる。現在までに墳丘（特に東側）は周囲の開発によって削られているほか、くびれ部西側には諏訪大社境外社の青塚社が建てられている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北北西に向ける。諏訪地方では唯一の前方後円墳で、前方後円墳としては末期に属する形態である{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]が認められるが、埴輪が出土した点でも本古墳が諏訪地方で唯一の例になる。[[江戸時代]]の史料によると武人埴輪が出土し埋め戻されたというが、近年の調査では円筒埴輪以外は見つかっていない。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部西側のくびれ部付近、基準面から5メートルほどの高さに露出・開口する史跡説明板。。[[安山岩]]の自然石による乱石積からなり、石室全長は5.5メートルを測る[http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳]（下諏訪町ホームページ）。。羨道は破壊されているが、両袖式の石室になると推測される{{Sfn|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}。石室の副葬品の出土はほとんど知られていない。 この青塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃の築造と推定される。諏訪大社下社の祭祀氏族（金刺氏）との関係のほか、[[天竜川]]沿いに分布する前方後円墳との関係を指摘する説が挙げられ、古代の諏訪地方の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 * 墳丘長：57メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：34メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8.1メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル（推定復元） ** 高さ：8メートル ただし現地説明板では、主軸長67メートル、後円部直径33.4メートル、前方部幅42.8メートルとする。 File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|くびれ部に青塚社（左）、後円部に石室（右上） File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|石室開口部 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 下諏訪青塚古墳 - 1965年（昭和40年）2月25日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=青塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|青塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] * [[金刺氏]] *[[下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|下諏町の名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 下諏訪青塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html 青塚古墳] - 下諏訪町ホームページ {{諏訪信仰}} {{デフォルトソート:しもすわあおつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]
