Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa|qq=}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryu River|qq=}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa District, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano|qq=}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa|qq=}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryu River|qq=}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa District, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano|qq=}} Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa|qq=}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryū River|lt=Tenryū River|lt=Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryu River|qq=}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]] Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. Its type is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|qq=}} of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryu River]]. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]

Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryu River]]. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:下諏訪町の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]] Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. Its type is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|qq=}} of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryu River]]. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[en:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[Category:下諏訪町の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]]

Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]] Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. Its type is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|qq=}} of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryu River]]. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]]

Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryu River]]. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} [[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]] Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. Its type is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryu River]]. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shimosuwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} [[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Mound (foreground: front part, top right: rear part) | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]] Aozuka 3340 | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 4 | coordinates = 34.97 | coordinates = 138 | coordinates = 5 | coordinates = 20.68 | omitted = JP-20 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - northwest }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. Its type is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural [[:en:Andesite|andesite]] stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryu River]]. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * {{ill|Kanemasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanemasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:下諏訪町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} [[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]]
