Its type is that of a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryu River}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}} * {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|1=Shimosuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1204211}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} [[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q20044844}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shimosuwa]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Its type is that of a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}}. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite|1=Andesite}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryu River|1=Tenryū River}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|1=Shimosuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1204211}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} [[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q20044844}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shimosuwa]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Kofun]][[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[simple:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG | location = {{ill|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|1=Shimosuwa}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.076381|N|138.089078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano|1=Shimosuwa}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite|1=Andesite}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryu River|1=Tenryū River}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|1=Shimosuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1204211}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} [[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q20044844}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shimosuwa]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[simple:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[simple:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]] [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]][[simple:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun]][[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun | image = File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun zenkei.JPG | location = {{ill|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|1=Shimosuwa}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.076381|N|138.089078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | map_size = Mound length: 57 m (or 67 m) | built = Late 6th century | burial = Double-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Deceased = Unknown | excavations = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, (traditionally) human haniwa | Cultural Historic Site = Nagano Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic site "Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun" | notes = | map_dot_label = Shimosuwa Aozuka
Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano|1=Shimosuwa}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The {{ill|Aozuka Shrine|ja|青塚社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aozuka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of Suwa Taisha, is located in the western constriction area of the mound. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite|1=Andesite}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryu River|1=Tenryū River}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters. File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun Aozuka-sha and sekishitsu.JPG|Constriction area with Aozuka Shrine (left) and stone chamber (top right) File:Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Entrance to the stone chamber == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun - Designated on February 25, 1965. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site Intypeation Board (Installed by Nagano Prefectural Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Aozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Aozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}} * {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa, Nagano#Landmarks|1=Shimosuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1204211}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2373&seq=0 Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun] - The Hachijuni Cultural Foundation * [http://www.town.shimosuwa.lg.jp/www/contents/1001000000743/index.html Aozuka Kofun] - Shimosuwa Town Website ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳|version=97369725|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun}} [[zh:下諏訪青塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q20044844}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shimosuwa]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Kofun | map = Japan Nagano | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Shimosuwa Aozuka Kofun''' (下諏訪青塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa, Nagano|1=Shimosuwa}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. Its type is that of a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and it is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Nagano Prefecture. == Overview == The Kofun was constructed on the tip of a hill projecting westward in the northern region of {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}}. It is situated near the {{ill|Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. The site is within the shrine's property, and parts of the mound (especially the eastern side) have been eroded by surrounding developments. The kofun is keyhole-shaped with its front part oriented to the north-northwest. It is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun in the Suwa region and belongs to the late phase of this style. {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been discovered on the mound's surface, making it the only example in the Suwa region where haniwa have been found. Historical records from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} mention the discovery of human-shaped haniwa, which were later reburied, though recent investigations have found no items other than cylindrical haniwa. The burial facility is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exposed and open on the western constriction area of the rear mound, approximately five meters above the base level. The chamber is constructed with natural {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite|1=Andesite}} stones in a random stone masonry style. It measures 5.5 meters in total length. Although the corridor leading to the chamber has been destroyed, it is presumed to have been a double-chambered type. Few burial goods from the chamber are known. The Aozuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century. Scholars suggest links to the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}}, the ritualist family of Suwa Taisha's Shimosha, and connections with other keyhole-shaped Kofuns distributed along the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryu River|1=Tenryū River}}. This makes the mound an important archaeological site for studying the political landscape of ancient Suwa. The Kofun was Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefecture historic site in 1965. == Mound == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: 57 meters * Rear part: ''' Diameter: 34 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8.1 meters * Front part: ''' Width: 40 meters (estimated reconstruction) ''' Height: 8 meters According to the site's intypeation board, the primary axis length is 67 meters, the rear part's diameter is 33.4 meters, and the front part's width is 42.8 meters.
